seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Freemans and Freemans Reach. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Freemans vs Freemans Reach: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Freemans and Freemans Reach.
heading: Freemans vs Freemans Reach — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Freemans, New South Wales and Freemans Reach, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Freemans Reach has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Freemans (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Freemans or Freemans Reach safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Freemans has a flood risk score of 21/100, while Freemans Reach scores 51/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Freemans scores 57/100 for bushfire risk. Freemans Reach scores 25/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Freemans or Freemans Reach?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Freemans Reach has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.85% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
